questions.question: What is the primary goal of the Software Requirements Specification (SRS)?
options: A) To design the software architecture
B) To define the user interface
C) To specify the functional and non-functional requirements
D) To estimate the project cost
questions.explanation: The primary goal of the SRS is to define the functional and non-functional requirements of the software, which serves as a blueprint for the project.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a well-designed software system?
options: A) High modularity
B) Low coupling
C) High cohesion
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: A well-designed software system should exhibit high modularity, low coupling, and high cohesion, making it easier to maintain, modify, and extend.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Use Case Diagram?
options: A) To design the software architecture
B) To define the user interface
C) To identify the main functions and interactions of the system
D) To estimate the project cost
questions.explanation: The Use Case Diagram is used to identify the main functions and interactions of the system, providing a high-level view of the system's behavior and functionality.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a non-functional requirement?
options: A) The system should be able to process 1000 transactions per second
B) The system should be able to run on Windows, Linux, and macOS
C) The system should be user-friendly and easy to use
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Non-functional requirements include performance, scalability, usability, and security, which are essential aspects of software development.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Requirements Traceability Matrix?
options: A) To design the software architecture
B) To define the user interface
C) To track the status of requirements and their fulfillment
D) To estimate the project cost
questions.explanation: The Requirements Traceability Matrix is used to track the status of requirements and their fulfillment, ensuring that all requirements are met and that changes are properly managed.
